summary_one_line: Input must place the exact 4-byte LUT tag signature (e.g. A2B0) in the ICC profile tag table so cmsIsTag(tag16) is true

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The ICC profile body must declare a tag-table entry whose 4-byte tag signature equals the runtime-selected tag16 (Device2PCS16[Intent], a LUT-based AToBx FOURCC). cmsIsTag walks the parsed tag directory comparing each stored signature against tag16, so the input bytes encoding a directory entry must contain that exact FOURCC.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry valid ICC header bytes (acsp magic, lcms signature, device-class FOURCCs like prtr/spac/mntr and colorspace tags RGB/CMY/Lab/XYZ) and the cmplog winner's ancestor chain shows I2S-floor dash rows, indicating I2SRandReplace spliced the logged tag16 FOURCC into a tag-table offset so the stored signature matches.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds (naive / value_profile) all show the false side: at line 355 L takes T=0 F=48, i.e. no stored tag signature ever equals tag16. Their havoc/token mutators never reconstruct the precise 4-byte FOURCC needed in the tag directory, so cmsIsTag stays false.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/value_profile_cmplog intercept the integer/string CMP inside cmsIsTag's signature comparison, log the expected tag16 FOURCC, and I2SRandReplace substitutes that exact 4-byte value into the input at a tag-directory offset. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and cannot land the exact magic, so they stay blocked.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is a tag-presence test cmsIsTag(hProfile, tag16) whose globally-blocked side is the true branch.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: if (cmsIsTag(hProfile, tag16)) { // Is there any LUT-Based table?

evidence_trail.claim: tag16 is a runtime-selected LUT tag signature derived from the intent index.
Winner inputs carry valid ICC profile structure with device-class and colorspace FOURCC fields.
Winner and loser seeds diverge precisely at the FOURCC bytes of the device-class / colorspace tag fields.
evidence_trail.cited_section: BYTE DIFF (W vs L at common offsets)

evidence_trail.claim: At line 355 the loser-blocking seeds take the false branch every time (T=0 F=48) while winner takes true.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: if (cmsIsTag(hProfile, tag16)) { // Is there any LUT-Base...  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog/vpc add I2SRandReplace which substitutes one logged CMP operand into matching input offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 2175 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 066b39714b4e5b64` and observed I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depths 1 and 2 of the ancestor chain, the lower-bound signal of an I2SRandReplace find under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the gated tag signature is fixed/absent so no CMP logs the FOURCC: if cmplog/vpc still resolve the branch as fast as on the real target, the I2S-substitution attribution is wrong.

weakest_evidence_point: The byte-diff shows FOURCC variation in the header/colorspace fields but does not directly pinpoint the offset of the tag-directory entry that I2S substitutes the LUT signature into.
